abstract: This paper by Terenzi and Giglietto, published in 2025, surveys the landscape of social media research APIs and tools in the context of the European Union's Digital Services Act (DSA). It situates this review within ongoing debates about platform data access for researchers following recent regulatory developments. (Summary based on title and metadata only, as no abstract or full text was provided.)
key_claims: The state of social media research APIs and tools has shifted meaningfully in the era of the Digital Services Act (specific claims not available without source text).
methods: Not specified in available metadata; likely a review or landscape assessment of available APIs and research tools given the title.
findings: Not available — no abstract or full text provided.
contributions: Provides a stocktaking of social media research infrastructure under the DSA regulatory regime (inferred from title).
framing: The paper positions itself within the intersection of platform governance, EU digital regulation (specifically the Digital Services Act), and the methodological infrastructure of computational social science and internet research.
